description:
# Extend reducer to descend into recursive calls under a budget

## Goal

Extend `ir_reducer` to follow recursive calls. Tracks an unroll
counter per top-level callsite; default budget = 32 reduction steps.

**All-or-nothing per top-level callsite:**
- If recursion terminates within budget → commit reduced IR.
- If budget is exceeded → discard partial work, leave the original
  call in place. The body stays a single call to a single body.

The all-or-nothing policy makes the residual predictable: no
half-unrolled tail-recursion artifacts; either fully reduced or
the original call.

## Stop conditions, by precedence

1. Input is opaque (Descr too wide; pattern matrix returns
   `StopOpaque` / `StopAmbiguous`).
2. Recursive call where structural decrease cannot be proven (the
   argument's Descr is not strictly smaller than the parent call's).
3. Recursive call where decrease IS provable but budget exceeded.

Conditions 1 and 2 take precedence over budget — budget is the
last resort, not the first.

## What structural decrease means

Provable shrinkage in at least one of:
- Tuple arity (fewer fields after projection).
- List length (cons step).
- Integer value monotonically toward a clause base (e.g. `n - 1`
  with literal `n > 0`).

If the argument is an opaque integer or a wide list type, decrease
is NOT provable — stop.

## Acceptance

- `ast_eval`: full reduction. main → `print(42); print(14); print(21)`.
  Zero `eval` bodies. (Or, if we keep per-clause splitting, ≤ 3.)
- `fib_tailrec(0|1|10|20)`: full reduction. Zero `fib` bodies.
- `mutual_recursion(0|1|7|10)`: full reduction. Zero `is_even`/`is_odd`
  bodies.
- `list_primitives` over the static `[1,2,3,4,5]`: full reduction.
  Zero `length`/`reverse`/`map`/`foldl` bodies.
- `tail_recursion` (count_100k): unchanged — 1 `count` body emitted.
  Budget exceeded → call left in place.

## Removals

`fz-ul4.11.28` (bounded recursion unrolling) is **subsumed**. Close
it with a link to this ticket on land.
title: fz-RED.4: recursive reduction with unroll budget (subsumes fz-ul4.11.28)
